pi_items.source: Web search results confirm 'Curbside' is a real, standard order type used in restaurant POS systems for prepaid contactless pickups. Toast POS and other commercial systems document curbside pickup as an order type. Floreant POS documentation confirms order types can be customized with user-defined names.

Floreant POS documentation (floreant.org and guide.orocube.com) explicitly lists 'Show Table Selection' as a configuration option for order types. export_result.sh script (line 22) queries this exact column from ORDER_TYPE table. Verifier.py (line 67) checks this field with value 0 (unchecked). Curbside orders do not require table selection, so 0 is logically correct.

Floreant POS documentation explicitly lists 'Show Guest Selection' as a configuration option (Number of guests in a group). export_result.sh script (line 22) queries this exact column. Verifier.py (line 75) checks this field. Curbside is a non-table-service model, so 0 is appropriate.

Floreant POS documentation states: 'Require Customer Data: Usually Home Delivery orders require to enter Customer Data before taking food order. It might be necessary for retail stores as well.' Curbside prepaid orders would require customer data for contactless delivery. export_result.sh queries this column. Verifier.py (line 51) checks this critical field. Value 1 (checked) is logically appropriate.

Floreant POS documentation confirms: 'Prepaid says customer must pay first and then take service.' Curbside order type explicitly for 'prepaid contactless pickups' per task description. export_result.sh queries this exact column. Verifier.py (line 59) checks this critical field. Value 1 (checked) is correct for the curbside use case.

Floreant POS uses Apache Derby embedded database. export_result.sh script uses Derby ij tool with jdbc:derby: connection strings. setup_floreant.sh confirms Derby is used. Audit document confirms Derby database at /opt/floreantpos/database/derby-server/posdb/

Floreant POS is real, open-source Java-based restaurant POS software. Founded 2008, maintained by OROCUBE LLC. Released under MRPL 1.2 license. Download available from SourceForge. GitHub repositories exist. Official documentation at floreant.org. Used by ~30,000 restaurants in 25 countries. Version in task is 1.4-build1707b.

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a real order type named 'Curbside' in Floreant POS, a genuine open-source Java restaurant POS system. The metadata configuration fields (SHOW_TABLE_SELECTION: 0, SHOW_GUEST_SELECTION: 0, REQUIRED_CUSTOMER_DATA: 1, PREPAID: 1) are all real features documented in Floreant POS. The values are logically correct for curbside prepaid orders: no table or guest selection (not table service), customer data required, and prepaid (customer must pay before pickup). The database uses Apache Derby with columns matching the task metadata exactly. 'Curbside' is a real order type designation used across modern POS systems (Toast, Square, etc.) for contactless prepaid pickups.
